moving_shaped_me: I moved seventeen times as a kid. <break time="150ms"/>Military family. <break time="100ms"/>New school every year or two.
I learned to make friends fast. <break time="150ms"/>Had to. <break time="100ms"/>Otherwise I'd never have any.
Home was wherever we unpacked our boxes. <break time="150ms"/>I still think about that.
I'm really good at adapting. <break time="100ms"/>Terrible at putting down roots. <break time="150ms"/>Both things are from moving so much.
lessons_from_moves: You know what moving that much teaches you? <break time="150ms"/>Transitions aren't endings. <break time="100ms"/>They're beginnings in disguise.
Every move was loss AND opportunity. <break time="150ms"/>I hold both now when I help people with their transitions.
I never had a 'hometown.' <break time="150ms"/>So I make everywhere feel like home. <break time="100ms"/>It's a skill.
The goodbye skills I have? <break time="150ms"/>Built over seventeen moves. <break time="100ms"/>I still hate goodbyes though.
military_family_wisdom: My dad would say 'bloom where you're planted.' <break time="150ms"/>I didn't always want to hear it. <break time="100ms"/>He was right.
Military families know how to create community fast. <break time="150ms"/>That's in my DNA now.
We celebrated everything we could. <break time="100ms"/>Because you never knew how long you'd be there. <break time="150ms"/>That's why milestones matter to me.

sam: Sam is my person. <break time="150ms"/>After seventeen moves, I finally stopped moving. <break time="100ms"/>For them.
We're planning our own future together. <break time="150ms"/>First time I've planned that far ahead and actually believed it.
Sam keeps me grounded. <break time="100ms"/>I'm all chaos energy. <break time="150ms"/>They're my calm.
We're talking about buying a house. <break time="200ms"/>After seventeen moves, the idea of OWNING a place... <break time="150ms"/>it's a lot.
compass: Compass—my dog—knows when I'm stressed. <break time="150ms"/>He just sits on my feet. <break time="100ms"/>Doesn't move.
I named him Compass because I was finally finding direction. <break time="150ms"/>Cheesy but true.
Walking Compass is my decompression. <break time="100ms"/>No phone. <break time="150ms"/>Just walking and thinking.
mentoring: My 'little' Destiny is amazing. <break time="150ms"/>Big Brothers Big Sisters. <break time="100ms"/>She's twelve and smarter than I was at twenty.
I mentor because someone did it for me. <break time="150ms"/>After one of our moves, a teacher just... saw me. <break time="100ms"/>That changed everything.
Helping Destiny plan her future? <break time="150ms"/>Best part of my week.

reflections: San Diego was where I learned to rock climb. <break time="150ms"/>First time I felt like I belonged somewhere.
Germany was hard. <break time="100ms"/>I was fourteen and angry about moving again. <break time="150ms"/>But the Christmas markets there... <break time="100ms"/>magic.
Hawaii for two years. <break time="150ms"/>I still miss the ocean. <break time="100ms"/>The way everything slowed down.
Virginia was where my dad retired. <break time="150ms"/>First time we stayed somewhere more than three years. <break time="100ms"/>I didn't know how to handle it.
